revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 多賀神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 愛媛県松山市新立町4-23 |位置 = |祭神 = 伊邪那岐命
伊邪那美命 |社格 = |創建 = 寛永5年（1628年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} [[画像:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg‎|thumb|240px|right|神紋（左三つ巴）]] '''多賀神社'''（たがじんじゃ） は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]である。神紋は左三つ巴。 ==祭神== *[[伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） *[[伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） ==由緒== *寛永5年（[[1628年]]）、[[蒲生忠知]]が[[松山藩|松山藩主]]になった際、蒲生氏の氏神である[[多賀大社]]（滋賀県多賀町）を勧請した。 *大正2年（[[1913年]]）、[[招魂社]]が[[愛媛県立農業学校]]の建設のため、境内に遷座された。 *昭和14年（1939年）4月、招魂社が内務大臣指定の[[愛媛縣護國神社]]となり、同年10月9日に現在地（松山市御幸町）に遷座した。 ==年中行事== {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 1月1日 ||[[歳旦祭]]   |- | 6月30日 || [[大祓|夏越祭]]   |- | 8月3日、4日 || 夏季大祭（献灯祭）   |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|秋季大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |- | 11月15日 || [[七五三]]   |} ==その他== *俳人である[[正岡子規]]直筆の奉納額を所蔵。 *白蛇逸話にまつわる[[八咫鏡|八呎の鏡]]を所蔵。 *関西屈指の大きさで、愛媛県内でも最大の大太鼓（延寿大太鼓）を所蔵。この太鼓は、旧温泉郡拝志村の神木で作成された2個の内の根元の部分で作られており、もう一方は[[大山祇神社]]（今治市大三島町）にある。 *一時期、招魂社（後の愛媛縣護國神社）が遷座されていたが、招魂社創建の際、宮司[[堀辯一郎]]の尽力によって招魂社に[[靖国神社]]の分霊を受けている。（これは靖国神社としても昭和44年（[[1969年]]）の創立100周年に資料を整理した際に事実が判明し、全国で唯一の例である。） ==アクセス== *[[伊予鉄道]]バス新立下車、徒歩2分 ==周辺施設== *[[金刀比羅宮松山分社]] *[[愛媛県立松山商業高等学校]] *[[松山市立八坂小学校]] *[[松山市立素鵞小学校]] *さくら幼稚園（境内に併設） == 関連項目 == *[[靖国神社]] *[[愛媛縣護國神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:松山市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 多賀神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Matsuyamatagajinja.JPG|250px|多賀神社正面]]
正面より |所在地 = 愛媛県松山市新立町4-23 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|50|12|N|132|46|43|E|}} |祭神 = 伊邪那岐命
伊邪那美命 |社格 = |創建 = 寛永5年（1628年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} [[画像:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg‎|thumb|240px|right|神紋（左三つ巴）]] '''多賀神社'''（たがじんじゃ） は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]である。神紋は左三つ巴。 == 祭神 == * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） == 由緒 == * 寛永5年（[[1628年]]）、[[蒲生忠知]]が[[松山藩|松山藩主]]になった際、蒲生氏の氏神である[[多賀大社]]（滋賀県多賀町）を勧請した。 * 大正2年（[[1913年]]）、[[招魂社]]が[[愛媛県立農業学校]]の建設のため、境内に遷座された。 * 昭和14年（1939年）4月、招魂社が内務大臣指定の[[愛媛縣護國神社]]となり、同年10月9日に現在地（松山市御幸町）に遷座した。 == 年中行事 == {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 1月1日 ||[[歳旦祭]]   |- | 6月30日 || [[大祓|夏越祭]]   |- | 8月3日、4日 || 夏季大祭（献灯祭）   |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|秋季大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |- | 11月15日 || [[七五三]]   |} == その他 == * 俳人である[[正岡子規]]直筆の奉納額を所蔵。 * 白蛇逸話にまつわる[[八咫鏡|八呎の鏡]]を所蔵。 * 関西屈指の大きさで、愛媛県内でも最大の大太鼓（延寿大太鼓）を所蔵。この太鼓は、旧温泉郡拝志村の神木で作成された2個の内の根元の部分で作られており、もう一方は[[大山祇神社]]（今治市大三島町）にある。 * 一時期、招魂社（後の愛媛縣護國神社）が遷座されていたが、招魂社創建の際、宮司[[堀辯一郎]]の尽力によって招魂社に[[靖国神社]]の分霊を受けている。（これは靖国神社としても昭和44年（[[1969年]]）の創立100周年に資料を整理した際に事実が判明し、全国で唯一の例である。） == アクセス == * [[伊予鉄道]]バス新立下車、徒歩2分 == 周辺施設 == * [[金刀比羅宮松山分社]] * [[愛媛県立松山商業高等学校]] * [[松山市立八坂小学校]] * [[松山市立素鵞小学校]] * さくら幼稚園（境内に併設） == 関連項目 == * [[靖国神社]] * [[愛媛縣護國神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:松山市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

伊邪那美命 |社格 = |創建 = 寛永5年（1628年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} [[画像:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg‎|thumb|240px|right|神紋（左三つ巴）]] '''多賀神社'''（たがじんじゃ） は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]である。神紋は左三つ巴。 == 祭神 == * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） == 由緒 == * 寛永5年（[[1628年]]）、[[蒲生忠知]]が[[伊予松山藩|松山藩]]主になった際、蒲生氏の氏神である[[多賀大社]]（滋賀県多賀町）を勧請した。 * 大正2年（[[1913年]]）、[[招魂社]]が[[愛媛県立農業学校]]の建設のため、境内に遷座された。 * 昭和14年（1939年）4月、招魂社が内務大臣指定の[[愛媛縣護國神社]]となり、同年10月9日に現在地（松山市御幸町）に遷座した。 == 年中行事 == {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 1月1日 ||[[歳旦祭]]   |- | 6月30日 || [[大祓|夏越祭]]   |- | 8月3日、4日 || 夏季大祭（献灯祭）   |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|秋季大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |- | 11月15日 || [[七五三]]   |} == その他 == * 俳人である[[正岡子規]]直筆の奉納額を所蔵。 * 白蛇逸話にまつわる[[八咫鏡|八呎の鏡]]を所蔵。 * 関西屈指の大きさで、愛媛県内でも最大の大太鼓（延寿大太鼓）を所蔵。この太鼓は、旧温泉郡拝志村の神木で作成された2個の内の根元の部分で作られており、もう一方は[[大山祇神社]]（今治市大三島町）にある。 * 一時期、招魂社（後の愛媛縣護國神社）が遷座されていた. == アクセス == * [[伊予鉄道]]バス新立下車、徒歩2分 == 周辺施設 == * [[金刀比羅宮松山分社]] * [[愛媛県立松山商業高等学校]] * [[松山市立八坂小学校]] * [[松山市立素鵞小学校]] * さくら幼稚園（境内に併設） == 関連項目 == * [[靖国神社]] * [[愛媛縣護國神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 多賀神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Matsuyamatagajinja.JPG|250px|多賀神社正面]]
正面より |所在地 = 愛媛県松山市新立町4-23 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|50|12|N|132|46|43|E|}} |祭神 = 伊邪那岐命
伊邪那美命 |社格 = |創建 = 寛永5年（1628年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} [[画像:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|神紋（[[左三つ巴]]）]] '''多賀神社'''（たがじんじゃ） は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]][[新立町]]にある[[神社]]である。神紋は左三つ巴。 == 祭神 == * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） == 由緒 == * 寛永5年（[[1628年]]）、[[蒲生忠知]]が[[伊予松山藩|松山藩]]主になった際、蒲生氏の氏神である[[多賀大社]]（滋賀県多賀町）を勧請した。 * 大正2年（[[1913年]]）、[[招魂社]]が[[愛媛県立農業学校]]の建設のため、境内に遷座された。 * 昭和14年（1939年）4月、招魂社が内務大臣指定の[[愛媛縣護國神社]]となり、同年10月9日に現在地（松山市御幸町）に遷座した。 == 年中行事 == {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 1月1日 ||[[歳旦祭]]   |- | 6月30日 || [[大祓|夏越祭]]   |- | 8月3日、4日 || 夏季大祭（献灯祭）   |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|秋季大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |- | 11月15日 || [[七五三]]   |} == その他 == * 俳人である[[正岡子規]]直筆の奉納額を所蔵。 * 白蛇逸話にまつわる[[八咫鏡|八呎の鏡]]を所蔵。 * 関西屈指の大きさで、愛媛県内でも最大の大太鼓（延寿大太鼓）を所蔵。この太鼓は、旧温泉郡拝志村の神木で作成された2個の内の根元の部分で作られており、もう一方は[[大山祇神社]]（今治市大三島町）にある。 * 一時期、招魂社（後の愛媛縣護國神社）が遷座されていた. == アクセス == * [[伊予鉄道]]バス新立下車、徒歩2分 == 周辺施設 == * [[金刀比羅宮松山分社]] * [[愛媛県立松山商業高等学校]] * [[松山市立八坂小学校]] * [[松山市立素鵞小学校]] * さくら幼稚園（境内に併設） == 関連項目 == * [[靖国神社]] * [[愛媛縣護國神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:多賀神社]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=愛媛県}}

{{神社 |名称 = 多賀神社 |画像 = [[File:Matsuyamatagajinja.JPG|250px|多賀神社正面]]
正面より |所在地 = 愛媛県松山市新立町4-23 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|50|12|N|132|46|43|E|}} |祭神 = 伊邪那岐命
伊邪那美命 |社格 = |創建 = 寛永5年（1628年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} [[画像:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|神紋（[[左三つ巴]]）]] '''多賀神社'''（たがじんじゃ） は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]][[新立町]]にある[[神社]]である。神紋は左三つ巴。 == 祭神 == * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） == 由緒 == * 寛永5年（[[1628年]]）、[[蒲生忠知]]が[[伊予松山藩|松山藩]]主になった際、蒲生氏の氏神である[[多賀大社]]（滋賀県多賀町）を勧請した。 * 大正2年（[[1913年]]）、[[招魂社]]が[[愛媛県立農業学校]]の建設のため、境内に遷座された。 * 昭和14年（1939年）4月、招魂社が内務大臣指定の[[愛媛縣護國神社]]となり、同年10月9日に現在地（松山市御幸町）に遷座した。 == 年中行事 == {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 1月1日 ||[[歳旦祭]]   |- | 6月30日 || [[大祓|夏越祭]]   |- | 8月3日、4日 || 夏季大祭（献灯祭）   |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|秋季大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |- | 11月15日 || [[七五三]]   |} == その他 == * 俳人である[[正岡子規]]直筆の奉納額を所蔵。 * 白蛇逸話にまつわる[[八咫鏡|八呎の鏡]]を所蔵。 * 関西屈指の大きさで、愛媛県内でも最大の大太鼓（延寿大太鼓）を所蔵。この太鼓は、旧温泉郡拝志村の神木で作成された2個の内の根元の部分で作られており、もう一方は[[大山祇神社]]（今治市大三島町）にある。 * 一時期、招魂社（後の愛媛縣護國神社）が遷座されていた. == アクセス == * [[伊予鉄バス]]新立バス停徒歩2分 == 周辺施設 == * [[金刀比羅宮松山分社]] * [[愛媛県立松山商業高等学校]] * [[松山市立八坂小学校]] * [[松山市立素鵞小学校]] * さくら幼稚園（境内に併設） == 関連項目 == * [[靖国神社]] * [[愛媛縣護國神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:Taga Shrines]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]][[Category:qq]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=愛媛県}}[[Category:Pages needing attribution]]

伊邪那美命 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = 寛永5年（1628年） |本殿 = |別名 = |festival = 10月6日 |神事 = }} [[image:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|神紋（[[左三つ巴]]）]] '''多賀神社'''（たがじんじゃ） は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]][[新立町]]にある[[神社]]である。神紋は左三つ巴。 == deity == * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） == 由緒 == * 寛永5年（[[1628年]]）、[[蒲生忠知]]が[[伊予松山藩|松山藩]]主になった際、蒲生氏の氏神である[[多賀大社]]（滋賀県多賀町）を勧請した。 * 大正2年（[[1913年]]）、[[招魂社]]が[[愛媛県立農業学校]]の建設のため、境内に遷座された。 * 昭和14年（1939年）4月、招魂社が内務大臣指定の[[愛媛縣護國神社]]となり、同年10月9日に現在地（松山市御幸町）に遷座した。 == 年中行事 == {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 1月1日 ||[[歳旦祭]]   |- | 6月30日 || [[大祓|夏越祭]]   |- | 8月3日、4日 || 夏季大祭（献灯祭）   |- | 10月6日 || [[festival|秋季大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |- | 11月15日 || [[七五三]]   |} == その他 == * 俳人である[[正岡子規]]直筆の奉納額を所蔵。 * 白蛇逸話にまつわる[[八咫鏡|八呎の鏡]]を所蔵。 * 関西屈指の大きさで、愛媛県内でも最大の大太鼓（延寿大太鼓）を所蔵。この太鼓は、旧温泉郡拝志村の神木で作成された2個の内の根元の部分で作られており、もう一方は[[大山祇神社]]（今治市大三島町）にある。 * 一時期、招魂社（後の愛媛縣護國神社）が遷座されていた. == アクセス == * [[伊予鉄バス]]新立バス停徒歩2分 == 周辺施設 == * [[金刀比羅宮松山分社]] * [[愛媛県立松山商業高等学校]] * [[松山市立八坂小学校]] * [[松山市立素鵞小学校]] * さくら幼稚園（境内に併設） == 関連項目 == * [[靖国神社]] * [[愛媛縣護國神社]] {{citation needed:たかしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:Taga Shrines]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]][[Category:qq]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=愛媛県}}[[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]][[Category:qq]]

伊邪那美命 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = 寛永5年（1628年） |本殿 = |別名 = |festival = 10月6日 |神事 = }} [[image:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|神紋（[[左三つ巴]]）]] '''多賀神社'''（たがじんじゃ） は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]][[新立町]]にある[[神社]]である。神紋は左三つ巴。 == deity == * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） == 由緒 == * 寛永5年（[[1628]]）、[[蒲生忠知]]が[[伊予松山藩|松山藩]]主になった際、蒲生氏の氏神である[[多賀大社]]（滋賀県多賀町）を勧請した。 * 大正2年（[[1913]]）、[[招魂社]]が[[愛媛県立農業学校]]の建設のため、境内に遷座された。 * 昭和14年（1939年）4月、招魂社が内務大臣指定の[[愛媛縣護國神社]]となり、同年10月9日に現在地（松山市御幸町）に遷座した。 == 年中行事 == {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 1月1日 ||[[歳旦祭]]   |- | 6月30日 || [[大祓|夏越祭]]   |- | 8月3日、4日 || 夏季大祭（献灯祭）   |- | 10月6日 || [[festival|秋季大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |- | 11月15日 || [[七五三]]   |} == その他 == * 俳人である[[正岡子規]]直筆の奉納額を所蔵。 * 白蛇逸話にまつわる[[八咫鏡|八呎の鏡]]を所蔵。 * 関西屈指の大きさで、愛媛県内でも最大の大太鼓（延寿大太鼓）を所蔵。この太鼓は、旧温泉郡拝志村の神木で作成された2個の内の根元の部分で作られており、もう一方は[[大山祇神社]]（今治市大三島町）にある。 * 一時期、招魂社（後の愛媛縣護國神社）が遷座されていた. == アクセス == * [[伊予鉄バス]]新立バス停徒歩2分 == 周辺施設 == * [[金刀比羅宮松山分社]] * [[愛媛県立松山商業高等学校]] * [[松山市立八坂小学校]] * [[松山市立素鵞小学校]] * さくら幼稚園（境内に併設） == See Also == * [[靖国神社]] * [[愛媛縣護國神社]] {{citation neededたかしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:Taga Shrines]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]][[Category:qq]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=愛媛県}}[[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]][[Category:qq]]

{{神社 |name = 多賀神社 |image = [[File:Matsuyamatagajinja.JPG|250px|多賀神社正面]]
正面より | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = 愛媛県松山市新立町4-23 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|50|12|N|132|46|43|E|}} |deity = 伊邪那岐命
伊邪那美命 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = |創建 = 寛永5年（1628年） |本殿 = |別名 = |festival = 10月6日 |神事 = }} [[image:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|神紋（[[左三つ巴]]）]] '''多賀神社'''（たがじんじゃ） は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]][[新立町]]にある[[神社]]である。神紋は左三つ巴。 == deity == * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） == 由緒 == * 寛永5年（[[1628]]）、[[蒲生忠知]]が[[伊予松山藩|松山藩]]主になった際、蒲生氏の氏神である[[多賀大社]]（滋賀県多賀町）を勧請した。 * 大正2年（[[1913]]）、[[招魂社]]が[[愛媛県立農業学校]]の建設のため、境内に遷座された。 * 昭和14年（1939年）4月、招魂社が内務大臣指定の[[愛媛縣護國神社]]となり、同年10月9日に現在地（松山市御幸町）に遷座した。 == 年中行事 == {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 1月1日 ||[[歳旦祭]]   |- | 6月30日 || [[大祓|夏越祭]]   |- | 8月3日、4日 || 夏季大祭（献灯祭）   |- | 10月6日 || [[festival|秋季大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |- | 11月15日 || [[七五三]]   |} == その他 == * 俳人である[[正岡子規]]直筆の奉納額を所蔵。 * 白蛇逸話にまつわる[[八咫鏡|八呎の鏡]]を所蔵。 * 関西屈指の大きさで、愛媛県内でも最大の大太鼓（延寿大太鼓）を所蔵。この太鼓は、旧温泉郡拝志村の神木で作成された2個の内の根元の部分で作られており、もう一方は[[大山祇神社]]（今治市大三島町）にある。 * 一時期、招魂社（後の愛媛縣護國神社）が遷座されていた. == アクセス == * [[伊予鉄バス]]新立バス停徒歩2分 == 周辺施設 == * [[金刀比羅宮松山分社]] * [[愛媛県立松山商業高等学校]] * [[松山市立八坂小学校]] * [[松山市立素鵞小学校]] * さくら幼稚園（境内に併設） == See Also == * [[靖国神社]] * [[愛媛縣護國神社]] {{citation neededたかしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:Taga Shrines]] [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]][[Category:11]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=愛媛県}}[[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:return the see also]]

伊邪那美命 |社格 = |創建 = 寛永5年（1628年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} [[画像:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|神紋（[[左三つ巴]]）]] '''多賀神社'''（たがじんじゃ） は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]][[新立町]]にある[[神社]]である。神紋は左三つ巴。 == 祭神 == * [[イザナギ|伊邪那岐命]]（いざなぎのみこと） * [[イザナミ|伊邪那美命]]（いざなみのみこと） == 由緒 == * 寛永5年（[[1628年]]）、[[蒲生忠知]]が[[伊予松山藩|松山藩]]主になった際、蒲生氏の氏神である[[多賀大社]]（滋賀県多賀町）を勧請した。 * 大正2年（[[1913年]]）、[[招魂社]]が[[愛媛県立農業学校]]の建設のため、境内に遷座された。 * 昭和14年（1939年）4月、招魂社が内務大臣指定の[[愛媛縣護國神社]]となり、同年10月9日に現在地（松山市御幸町）に遷座した。 == 年中行事 == {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 1月1日 ||[[歳旦祭]]   |- | 6月30日 || [[大祓|夏越祭]]   |- | 8月3日、4日 || 夏季大祭（献灯祭）   |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|秋季大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |- | 11月15日 || [[七五三]]   |} == その他 == * 俳人である[[正岡子規]]直筆の奉納額を所蔵。 * 白蛇逸話にまつわる[[八咫鏡|八呎の鏡]]を所蔵。 * 関西屈指の大きさで、愛媛県内でも最大の大太鼓（延寿大太鼓）を所蔵。この太鼓は、旧温泉郡拝志村の神木で作成された2個の内の根元の部分で作られており、もう一方は[[大山祇神社]]（今治市大三島町）にある。 * 一時期、招魂社（後の愛媛縣護國神社）が遷座されていた. == アクセス == * [[伊予鉄バス]]新立バス停徒歩2分 == 周辺施設 == * [[金刀比羅宮松山分社]] * [[愛媛県立松山商業高等学校]] * [[松山市立八坂小学校]] * [[松山市立素鵞小学校]] * さくら幼稚園（境内に併設） == 関連項目 == * [[靖国神社]] * [[愛媛縣護國神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:たかしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:多賀神社]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=愛媛県}}

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine |name = Tagajinja (多賀神社) |image = 250px; {{ill|File:Matsuyamatagajinja.JPG|ja|多賀神社正面|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Matsuyamatagajinja.JPG|qq=}}
From the front |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama City, Shinritsu-cho 4-23 |coordinates = {{coord|33|50|12|N|132|46|43|E}} |deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto
Izanami-no-Mikoto |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |founded = Kan'ei 5 (1628) |main_shrine = |alias = |festival = October 6 |rituals = |image = File:Matsuyamatagajinja.JPG }} [[File:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Divine Emblem ({{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|qq=}})]] '''Tagajinja''' (多賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama City]], {{ill|Shinritsu-cho|ja|新立町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinritsu-cho|qq=}}. The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|qq=}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami-no-Mikoto]] == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ({{ill|1628|ja|1628|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1628|qq=}}), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|qq=}} became the lord of [[:en:Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|Matsuyama Domain]], he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga Taisha]] (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1913|qq=}}), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|qq=}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|qq=}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|qq=}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|qq=}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|ja|愛媛県立松山商業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja|松山市立八坂小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|ja|松山市立素鵞小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|qq=}} * Sakura Kindergarten (attached to the shrine grounds) == See Also == * [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Ehime Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|多賀神社 (松山市)|version=68254676|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Taga Shrines]] [[Category:Return the See Also]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] }} [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] [[ja:多賀神社]]

Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|qq=}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|qq=}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|qq=}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|ja|愛媛県立松山商業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja|松山市立八坂小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|ja|松山市立素鵞小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|qq=}} * Sakura Kindergarten (attached to the shrine grounds) == See Also == * [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Ehime Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|多賀神社 (松山市)|version=68254676|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Taga Shrines]] [[Category:Return the See Also]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] }} [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] [[ja:多賀神社]] [[Category:松山市の神社]]

{{Shinto Shrine |name = Tagajinja (多賀神社) |image = 250px; {{ill|File:Matsuyamatagajinja.JPG|ja|多賀神社正面|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Matsuyamatagajinja.JPG|qq=}}
From the front |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama City, Shinritsu-cho 4-23 |coordinates = {{coord|33|50|12|N|132|46|43|E}} |deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto
Izanami-no-Mikoto |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |founded = Kan'ei 5 (1628) |main_shrine = |alias = |festival = October 6 |rituals = |image = File:Matsuyamatagajinja.JPG }} [[File:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Divine Emblem ({{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|qq=}})]] '''Tagajinja''' (多賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama City]], {{ill|Shinritsu-cho|ja|新立町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinritsu-cho|qq=}}. The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|qq=}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami-no-Mikoto]] == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ({{ill|1628|ja|1628|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1628|qq=}}), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|qq=}} became the lord of [[:en:Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|Matsuyama Domain]], he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga Taisha]] (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1913|qq=}}), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|qq=}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|qq=}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami-no-Mikoto]] == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ({{ill|1628|ja|1628|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1628|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1000}}), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|qq=}} became the lord of [[:en:Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|Matsuyama Domain]], he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga Taisha]] (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ({{ill|1913|ja|1913|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1913|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1000}}), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|qq=}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|qq=}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|qq=}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|ja|愛媛県立松山商業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|qq=}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|qq=}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|qq=}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|ja|愛媛県立松山商業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja|松山市立八坂小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|ja|松山市立素鵞小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|qq=}} * Sakura Kindergarten (attached to the shrine grounds) == See Also == * [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Ehime Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|多賀神社 (松山市)|version=68254676|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Taga Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] }} [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] [[ja:多賀神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]]

Izanami-no-Mikoto |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |founded = Kan'ei 5 (1628) |main_shrine = |alias = |festival = October 6 |rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022}} }} [[File:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Divine Emblem ({{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴}})]] '''Tagajinja''' (多賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama City]], {{ill|Shinritsu-cho|ja|新立町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinritsu-cho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami-no-Mikoto]] == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ([[:en:1628|1628]]), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|qq=}} became the lord of [[:en:Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|Matsuyama Domain]], he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga Taisha]] (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|qq=}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|qq=}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami-no-Mikoto]] == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ([[:en:1628|1628]]), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|qq=|1=Gamō Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8513824}} became the lord of [[:en:Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|Matsuyama Domain]], he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga Taisha]] (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=Ehimeken-Gokoku-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|qq=|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=Ehimeken-Gokoku-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|qq=|1=Iyotetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q53308357}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|qq=|1=Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646484}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|ja|愛媛県立松山商業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|qq=|1=Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493651}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja|松山市立八坂小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|ja|松山市立素鵞小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|qq=|1=Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529845}} * Sakura Kindergarten (attached to the shrine grounds) == See Also == * [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=Ehimeken-Gokoku-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}} {{Shinto-stub}} Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] }} [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] [[ja:多賀神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] Izanami-no-Mikoto |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |founded = Kan'ei 5 (1628) |main_shrine = |alias = |festival = October 6 |rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022}} }} [[File:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Divine Emblem ({{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}})]] '''Tagajinja''' (多賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama City]], {{ill|Shinritsu-cho|ja|新立町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinritsu-cho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami-no-Mikoto]] == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ([[:en:1628|1628]]), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|qq=|1=Gamō Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8513824}} became the lord of [[:en:Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|Matsuyama Domain]], he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga Taisha]] (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=Ehimeken-Gokoku-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|qq=|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=Ehimeken-Gokoku-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|qq=|1=Iyotetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q53308357}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|qq=|1=Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646484}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|ja|愛媛県立松山商業高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|qq=|1=Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493651}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja|松山市立八坂小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|ja|松山市立素鵞小学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|qq=|1=Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529845}} * Sakura Kindergarten (attached to the shrine grounds) == See Also == * [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=Ehimeken-Gokoku-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}} {{Shinto-stub}} The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami-no-Mikoto]] == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ([[:en:1628|1628]]), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|qq=|1=Gamō Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8513824}} became the lord of [[:en:Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|Matsuyama Domain]], he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga Taisha]] (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|qq=|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|qq=|1=Iyotetsu Izanami-no-Mikoto |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |founded = Kan'ei 5 (1628) |main_shrine = |alias = |festival = October 6 |rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022}} }} [[File:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Divine Emblem ({{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}})]] '''Tagajinja''' (多賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama City]], {{ill|Shinritsu-cho|ja|新立町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinritsu-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami-no-Mikoto]] == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ([[:en:1628|1628]]), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|1=Gamō Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8513824}} became the lord of [[:en:Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|Matsuyama Domain]], he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga Taisha]] (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|1=Iyotetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q53308357}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami-no-Mikoto]] == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ([[:en:1628|1628]]), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|1=Gamō Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8513824}} became the lord of [[:en:Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|Matsuyama Domain]], he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga Taisha]] (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|1=Iyotetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q53308357}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|1=Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646484}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|ja|愛媛県立松山商業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|1=Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493651}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja|松山市立八坂小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|ja|松山市立素鵞小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529845}} * Sakura The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami-no-Mikoto]] == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ([[:en:1628|1628]]), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|1=Gamō Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8513824}} became the lord of [[:en:Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|Matsuyama Domain]], he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga Taisha]] (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|1=Iyotetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q53308357}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|1=Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646484}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|ja|愛媛県立松山商業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|1=Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493651}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja|松山市立八坂小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|ja|松山市立素鵞小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529845}} * Sakura Kindergarten (attached to the shrine grounds) == See Also == * [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Ehime Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|多賀神社 Izanami-no-Mikoto |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |founded = Kan'ei 5 (1628) |main_shrine = |alias = |festival = October 6 |rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022}} }} [[File:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Divine Emblem ({{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}})]] '''Tagajinja''' (多賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama City]], {{ill|Shinritsu-cho|ja|新立町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinritsu-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami-no-Mikoto]] == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ([[:en:1628|1628]]), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|1=Gamō Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8513824}} became the lord of [[:en:Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|Matsuyama Domain]], he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga Taisha]] (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|1=Iyotetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q53308357}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|1=Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646484}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|ja|愛媛県立松山商業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|1=Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493651}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja|松山市立八坂小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|ja|松山市立素鵞小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529845}} * Sakura Kindergarten (attached to the shrine grounds) == See Also == * [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Izanami-no-Mikoto |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type = |founded = Kan'ei 5 (1628) |main_shrine = |alias = |festival = October 6 |rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022}} }} [[File:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Divine Emblem ({{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}})]] '''Tagajinja''' (多賀神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]], [[:en:Matsuyama|Matsuyama City]], {{ill|Shinritsu-cho|ja|新立町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinritsu-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami-no-Mikoto]] == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ([[:en:1628|1628]]), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|1=Gamō Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8513824}} became the lord of [[:en:Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|Matsuyama Domain]], he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, [[:en:Taga-taisha|Taga Taisha]] (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ([[:en:1913|1913]]), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}}   |- | June 30 || [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Summer Purification Festival]]   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}   |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkosai]]   |- | November 15 || [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]]   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet [[:en:Masaoka Shiki|Masaoka Shiki]]. * It houses the [[:en:Yata no Kagami|Eight-Foot Mirror]] associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at [[:en:Ōyamazumi Shrine|Ōyamazumi Shrine]] (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|1=Iyotetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q53308357}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|1=Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646484}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|ja|愛媛県立松山商業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|1=Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493651}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja|松山市立八坂小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|ja|松山市立素鵞小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529845}} * Sakura Kindergarten (attached to the shrine grounds) == See Also == * [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Ehime Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|多賀神社 Izanami-no-Mikoto |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |type = |founded = Kan'ei 5 (1628) |main_shrine = |alias = |festival = October 6 |rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022}} }} [[File:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Divine Emblem ({{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}})]] '''Tagajinja''' (多賀神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City}}, {{ill|Shinritsu-cho|ja|新立町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinritsu-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}} == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ({{ill|1628|en|1628|WD=Q6840|lt=1628}}), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|1=Gamō Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8513824}} became the lord of {{ill|Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|en|Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama Domain}}, he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, {{ill|Taga-taisha|en|Taga-taisha|WD=Q402091|lt=Taga Taisha}} (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ({{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913}}), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}}   |- | June 30 || {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|WD=Q11261910|lt=Summer Purification Festival}}   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}   |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|WD=Q123907414|lt=Shinkosai}}   |- | November 15 || {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}}   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet {{ill|Masaoka Shiki|en|Masaoka Shiki|WD=Q315152|lt=Masaoka Shiki}}. * It houses the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Eight-Foot Mirror}} associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|1=Iyotetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q53308357}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|1=Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646484}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|ja|愛媛県立松山商業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|1=Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493651}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja|松山市立八坂小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|ja|松山市立素鵞小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529845}} * Sakura Kindergarten (attached to the shrine grounds) == See Also == * {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Ehime Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|多賀神社 (松山市)|version=68254676|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] }} [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] [[ja:多賀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11431091}}
