[[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] {{Infobox religious building| |name = Tagajinja (多賀神社) |image = File:Matsuyamatagajinja.JPG |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |location = Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama City, Shinritsu-cho 4-23 |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83667|N|132.77861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto
Izanami-no-Mikoto |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |type = |founded = Kan'ei 5 (1628) |main_shrine = |alias = |festival = October 6 |rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022}} }} [[File:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Divine Emblem ({{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}})]] '''Tagajinja''' (多賀神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City}}, {{ill|Shinritsu-cho|ja|新立町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinritsu-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}} == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ({{ill|1628|en|1628|WD=Q6840|lt=1628}}), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|1=Gamō Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8513824}} became the lord of {{ill|Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|en|Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama Domain}}, he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, {{ill|Taga-taisha|en|Taga-taisha|WD=Q402091|lt=Taga Taisha}} (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ({{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913}}), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}}   |- | June 30 || {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|WD=Q11261910|lt=Summer Purification Festival}}   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}   |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|WD=Q123907414|lt=Shinkosai}}   |- | November 15 || {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}}   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet {{ill|Masaoka Shiki|en|Masaoka Shiki|WD=Q315152|lt=Masaoka Shiki}}. * It houses the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Eight-Foot Mirror}} associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2‑minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|1=Iyotetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q53308357}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|ja|金刀比羅宮松山分社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|1=Kotohira Shrine Matsuyama Branch|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646484}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|ja|愛媛県立松山商業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|1=Ehime Prefectural Matsuyama Commercial High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493651}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja|松山市立八坂小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yatsuzaka Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|ja|松山市立素鵞小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Soga Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529845}} * Sakura Kindergarten (attached to the shrine grounds) == See Also == * {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}} * {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Ehime Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|多賀神社 (松山市)|version=68254676|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] }} [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] [[ja:多賀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11431091}} [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] [[ja:多賀神社 (松山市)]] {{Infobox religious building| |name = Tagajinja (多賀神社) |image = File:Matsuyamatagajinja.JPG |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |location = Ehime Prefecture, Matsuyama City, Shinritsu-cho 4-23 |coordinates = {{Coord|33.83667|N|132.77861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Izanagi-no-Mikoto
Izanami-no-Mikoto |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |type = |founded = Kan'ei 5 (1628) |main_shrine = |alias = |festival = October 6 |rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|1=Matsuyama}} }} [[File:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Divine Emblem ({{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=Tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}})]] '''Tagajinja''' (多賀神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Shinritsu-cho|ja|新立町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinritsu-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The divine emblem is {{ill|Left Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=Tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1557435}}. == Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} == History == * In Kan'ei 5 ({{ill|1628|en|1628|WD=Q6840|lt=1628|1=1628}}), when {{ill|Gamo Tadayoshi|ja|蒲生忠知|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo Tadayoshi|1=Gamō Tadatomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8513824}} became the lord of {{ill|Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|en|Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama Domain|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}}, he enshrined his family's tutelary deity, {{ill|Taga-taisha|en|Taga-taisha|WD=Q402091|lt=Taga Taisha|1=Taga-taisha}} (Taga, Shiga Prefecture). * In Taishō 2 ({{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913|1=1913}}), {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was relocated to the shrine grounds for the construction of the {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja|愛媛県立農業学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Agricultural School|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * In Shōwa 14 (1939), in April, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} was designated by the Minister of Home Affairs as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}, and on October 9 of the same year, it was relocated to its current location (Miyuki-cho, Matsuyama City). == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | January 1 || {{ill|New Year's Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}}   |- | June 30 || {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|WD=Q11261910|lt=Summer Purification Festival|1=Ōharae-shiki}}   |- | August 3-4 || Summer Grand Festival (Lamp Offering Festival)   |- | October 6 || {{ill|Festival|ja|festival|lt=Autumn Grand Festival|lt_ja=秋季大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q304952}}   |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|WD=Q123907414|lt=Shinkosai|1=Shinko-shiki}}   |- | November 15 || {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San|1=Shichi-Go-San}}   |} == Other == * It holds a dedication plaque in the autograph of the haiku poet {{ill|Masaoka Shiki|en|Masaoka Shiki|WD=Q315152|lt=Masaoka Shiki|1=Masaoka Shiki}}. * It houses the {{ill|Yata no Kagami|en|Yata no Kagami|WD=Q2610017|lt=Eight-Foot Mirror|1=Yata no Kagami}} associated with the white snake legend. * It possesses one of the largest taiko drums in the Kansai region, and the largest in Ehime Prefecture (Enju Taiko). This drum was crafted from the root portion of one of two sacred trees from the former Onsen District, Haishi Village, with the other located at {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|en|Ōyamazumi Shrine|WD=Q703904|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}} (Ōmishima-cho, Imabari City). * For a period, {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Shokonsha|ja|招魂社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokonsha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (later known as {{ill|Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|愛媛縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|1=Ehime Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493796}}) was enshrined here. == Access == * A 2-minute walk from {{ill|Iyo Tetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tetsu Bus|1=Iyotetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q53308357}} Shinritsu Bus Stop == Nearby Facilities == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine
